set.office: State Senator (SD-28 · FL Senate presiding officer 2022-2024; term-limited, not seeking reelection 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-28 (Collier/Hendry + part of Lee) senator since 2016 (House 2010-2016, HD-106); President of the FL Senate 2022-2024 (succeeded by Ben Albritton). CURRENCY: term ends Nov 3, 2026 and she is TERM-LIMITED — not on the 2026 ballot. Religion not publicly confirmed (Roman Catholic plausible given Naples but no cited source — left unset, not inferred). Scored from her documented leadership role driving SB300/HB1069/HB1, a HARD 2018 roll call (SB7026), and floor advocacy; party-line marquee bills she presided over as the majority leader are scored on the recorded side per the marquee-vote rule.
profile.background: Born Jersey City NJ; Trinity College (DC) 1975; Stetson Law J.D. 1978. Naples real-estate/banking attorney (Kelly, Passidomo & Alba), board-certified in real-estate law; past president of the Collier County Bar.

kathleen-passidomo@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Conservative Senate President. HARD/leadership: drove and supported SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban) — sought the rape/incest/trafficking exceptions; advanced HB1069 (2023) expanding the classroom-instruction ban to pre-K-8 and barring compelled pronoun use; championed HB1 (2023) universal school choice ('one of the most transformative education bills in the history of the state'); supported HB543 (2023 permitless carry), spoke for it on the floor (Senate 27-13). Immigration: SB1718 (2023, E-Verify for 25+ employees) passed the Senate party-line under her presidency. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (2A): voted YEA on the 2018 Parkland SB7026 (Marjory Stoneman Douglas Act — age-21 long-gun, 3-day wait, bump-stock ban, created red-flag orders; roll call lists 'Passidomo-28 Y') and as Senate President opposed lowering the gun-buying age back to 18 and let HB759 stall — scored FALSE on self_defense q1. NOTE: the Andrew Warren suspension proceeding (Aug 2022) occurred under President Wilton Simpson, not her. Christian-liberty, economics, elections, and 10th-Amendment cells returned no individual evidence and are null.

0.note: As Senate President drove and supported SB300 (2023) 6-week heartbeat ban — pro-life, early-protection record

1.note: Supported a 6-week restriction and personally sought rape/incest/trafficking exceptions — abolition bar not met

4.note: No documented abortion-industry money; not inferred

2.note: As Senate President advanced HB1069 (2023) barring compelled pronoun use and affirming biological sex; voted for 2022 Parental Rights in Education — rejects transgender-ideology policy

4.note: Supported HB1557 Parental Rights in Education (2022) and the HB1069 expansion — opposes promotion of LGBTQ identity in schools

0.note: Championed HB1 (2023) universal school choice as Senate President — pro-school-choice

2.note: Advanced HB1069 (2023) expanding the ban on classroom instruction on sexual orientation/gender identity to pre-K-8 — opposes SOGI/gender-ideology curricula

3.note: HB3 porn-age-verification individual vote not isolated

0.note: CBDC ban (SB7054) passed under her presidency but no individual position located
Praised HB543 school-safety provisions but no affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar

set.office: State Senator (SD-29 · won re-election Nov-2024, 4-yr term next up 2028)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-29 (Glades/Highlands/Indian River/Okeechobee + part of St. Lucie) senator since 2022 (House 2016-2022, HD-54). CURRENCY: won re-election Nov 5, 2024 to a 4-year term — next up 2028, NOT on the 2026 ballot. Chairs Children/Families/Elder Affairs. Florida's most prominent legislative sponsor of pro-life law. Scored from HARD sponsorships (SB300, HB3, SB1044, SB1334), a 2018 Parkland roll call, and a sanctuary-ban co-sponsorship. NRA-endorsed; her individual HB543 (2023) vote was not independently confirmed, so the carry cell is left null rather than inferred.
profile.background: Vero Beach native; Wake Forest B.A. 1999, Marquette J.D. 2003; attorney (Grall Law Group). Married, three children. Entered the FL House in 2016 and the Senate in 2022.

erin-grall@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Uniformly conservative pro-life sponsor; no cross-party (liberal) deviation found. HARD sponsorships: SB300 (2023 6-week 'Heartbeat Protection Act' — Senate sponsor), the 15-week ban (HB5 2022, House sponsor), the Parental Notice of and Consent for Abortion Act (signed 2020), and a 2021 disability-abortion-prohibition bill; Florida Right to Life PAC-endorsed. Sponsored the 2023 biological-sex/pronoun expansion ('sex is an immutable biological trait') and voted YES on HB1557 Parental Rights in Education (2022). Sponsored HB3 (2024) under-14 social-media ban + porn-site age-verification, and the 2021 Parents' Bill of Rights. Sponsored SB1044 (2024) school chaplains and SB1334 (2026) documentary-proof-of-citizenship voter-roll maintenance. As a Rep, co-sponsored HB527 sanctuary-cities ban with Cord Byrd. PRO-2A note (not a liberal deviation): voted AGAINST the 2018 Parkland gun-control package — scored as opposing red-flag/age restrictions (self_defense q1). Economics, 10th-Amendment, and public-justice cells returned no evidence and are null.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-29 (Deltona/Volusia) Republican; first elected to the House Nov 2020 (then HD-27), moved to HD-29 after 2022 redistricting; re-elected Nov 5, 2024; term ends Nov 3, 2026 (on the 2026 ballot, campaign site active). webster-barnaby@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Conservative; no cross-party (liberal) deviation. HARD: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban); YEA Parental Rights in Education (2022); during April 2023 debate on HB1521 (trans bathroom / Safety in Private Spaces Act) called transgender speakers 'demons and imps' and 'mutants' (apologized same day) — documented rejection of transgender ideology. Pastor; sponsored HB1027 allowing student speakers (incl. prayer) before championship games and HB95 (security services on religious premises). Economics: YEA gold & silver as legal tender (2025) and YEA prohibiting UN-backed climate intervention (2025). Federal overreach: YEA COVID-19 mandate prohibition (2021). 2A: co-introducer + YEA HB543 (2023 permitless carry), repeatedly YEA to lower the long-gun age to 18 and YEA 2A financial privacy (2023). DEVIATION FLAG (left null, not scored against him): voted NO on expanding tax-credit scholarships (2023) and NO on state budgets (2022/2024) — a fiscal/limited-government posture, not a documented anti-school-choice position, so family q0 is left null pending intent. Election-integrity, public-justice, and immigration specifics returned no individual roll call and are null.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-30 (Boca Raton / Palm Beach-Broward) senator since 2020 (House 2018-2020, HD-81). CURRENCY: term ends Nov 3, 2026; as of May 2026 she said she is 'weighing all options' and has not committed to a 2026 reelection run — candidacy_status set to 'incumbent' rather than asserting seeking-reelection. Prior site record was an all-FALSE party-default placeholder citing only landing pages; replaced here with cited votes/endorsements. Scored from HARD roll calls, sponsorships, and group endorsements.
profile.background: Born 1968, Merrick NY; B.A. Univ. of Pennsylvania, J.D. Columbia; attorney. Served in the FL House 2018-2020 before winning her state Senate seat in 2020; focuses on reproductive rights, gun safety, and LGBTQ protections.

tina-scott-polsky@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Solidly progressive Palm Beach Democrat; NO cross-party (conservative) deviation found. Sanctity: voted NAY on SB300 (2023 6-week ban) and NAY on the 15-week ban (HB5 2022); sponsored SB288 (2025) to widen the exceptions; endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood PAC, EMILY's List, and Ruth's List; serves on the Reproductive Freedom Leadership Council. Marriage/sexuality: opposed the 2023 anti-trans bathroom bill ('designed to just hurt trans people') and the Parental Rights expansion ('you can't legislate away the gay'); Equality Florida-endorsed; defended Disney over its LGBTQ stance. Elections: voted NO on SB7050 (2023 omnibus elections restrictions). Guns: voted NO on HB543 (2023 permitless carry, 'most cynical political ploy') and offered amendments capping magazines at 16 rounds. No documented votes located on Christian-liberty, economics, public-justice, 10th-Amendment, or the SB264 foreign-land cell — those are null, not inferred.
